this will be an nmp app (../nostr-multi-platform) -- start setting up the basics -- we'll start with an ios and android app (that MUST be kept in feature-parity at all times) -- add an AGENTS.md rule like the one in nmp repo about 300/500 LOC soft/hard limit per source code file

Done. Here's what was set up:

**`AGENTS.md`** — contains the 300/500 LOC file-size rule (soft/hard), plus a **non-negotiable iOS/Android feature parity** section: every PR that touches one platform must ship the equivalent change on the other in the same PR, no stubs allowed.

**Project structure:**
```
Olas/
├── AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / WIP.md (gitignored)
├── Cargo.toml          — workspace, NMP crates pinned at v0.8.0 rev
├── justfile            — just rust-ios-sim, build-ios-sim, build-android, relay-run
├── apps/olas/
│   └── nmp-app-olas/   — app Rust crate (staticlib FFI for iOS/Android)
├── ios/Olas/
│   ├── project.yml     — XcodeGen, bundle io.f7z.olas, team 456SHKPP26
│   └── Olas/           — SwiftUI app shell (App/, Features/, Bridge/, etc.)
├── android/
│   └── app/            — Kotlin/Compose app (io.f7z.olas), cargo-ndk wired into preBuild
└── relay/
    ├── go.mod          — module github.com/pablof7z/olas/relay
    ├── main.go         — minimal HTTP/Nostr scaffold
    └── AGENTS.md       — relay-specific contributor notes
```

The `Cargo.toml` has the `[patch]` block for `../nostr-multi-platform` commented in ready to uncomment for local NMP development. Run `just gen-ios` to generate the Xcode project once `xcodegen` is available.
